text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Evergreen, there was a girl named Mildred. She loved nothing better than exploring the woods nearby. On a day with a gentle breeze, Mildred followed a narrow path between rows of ancient oaks.

Deep in thought, she glanced behind her. That's when she saw it—the most magnificent rose bush she had ever seen. It was overflowing with roses of every color! She remembered her grandmother telling her stories about hidden treasures. Maybe this was one.

With excitement, Mildred climbed up. The roses were soft against her cheek. She picked one. It smelled divine.

Just then, she heard rustling. Someone was there! Quietly, she crept toward the sound. It came closer. A fox! But soft!

She reached a clearing. There stood Merlin—not the wizard. He was big and furry. One big orange paw caught hers. He gave her a squeeze.

"Merry Christmas," said Merlin softly.

She laughed. She
